trivia_bullets.text: Fall from Favor is a CMR-original card (Scryfall: reprint: false) and carries no flavor text. It is one of two CMR commons in this wave that grant the Monarch — the other being Azure Fleet Admiral (CMR 58). The Monarch mechanic was brought to common rarity for the first time in CMR, a deliberate design choice to make it a recurring draft-format theme rather than a rare luxury.

Fall from Favor is banned in Pauper, the only format where a common could create a format-warping issue. Wizards announced the ban on January 14, 2021 — less than two months after CMR's November 20, 2020 release. The ruling cited Fall from Favor's ability to simultaneously remove a blocker, gain the Monarch (card draw engine), and lock that creature out of attacking as long as its controller isn't the Monarch. It compressed two distinct powerful effects into one three-mana common Aura, pushing out aggressive strategies from the Pauper metagame. It remains legal in Commander, Legacy, Vintage, and PauperCommander.
sources: WebSearch: Star City Games Fall From Favor Banned in Pauper
WebSearch: magic.wizards.com January 14 2021 Banned and Restricted Announcement

trivia_bullets.text: The Scryfall all_parts field lists Fall from Favor as a combo_piece linked to The Monarch token, confirming it is a designed component of CMR's Monarch theme cluster alongside Azure Fleet Admiral and the Court cycle.

Artist Caio Monteiro's composition — a robed figure staggering under a blue magical burst in front of a castle keep — visualizes the flavor concept of political disgrace rendered as physical curse. The card was previewed by MTG Muddstah (YouTube) on October 30, 2020, per the Scryfall preview field.
sources: Scryfall: preview source field — MTG Muddstah YouTube
